title: NGOs: Millions in Sudan Surviving on One Meal a Day as Food Crisis Deepens
detail: A coalition of NGOs reported on April 13 that millions of Sudanese civilians are surviving on just one meal a day as the civil war enters its third year, with violence and siege tactics systematically eroding Sudan's food supply chains. Families in North Darfur and South Kordofan are resorting to eating leaves and animal feed for survival. The report underscores how the ongoing SAF-RSF conflict has created one of the world's worst humanitarian crises, with humanitarian access severely restricted across conflict zones.

media.caption: Millions of Sudanese civilians surviving on one meal a day as food crisis deepens
